news.summary: Plains All American Pipeline, L.P. (NASDAQ: PAA) saw a decline in share prices as they released their first-quarter earnings report. They reported a revenue of $12.011 billion, falling short of the expected $14.26 billion consensus. The adjusted earnings per share (EPS) stood at 39 cents, below the anticipated 43 cents. However, the company's adjusted EBITDA, attributable to PAA, demonstrated a 5% year-over-year increase, reaching $754 million. The Crude Oil Segment's adjusted EBITDA also saw growth, rising by 1% year-over-year to $559 million, attributed to higher tariff volumes, escalations, and gains from acquisitions. Additionally, the NGL segment's adjusted EBITDA surged by 19%. For more details, refer to the article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Plains All American Pipeline's Q1 revenue was $12.011B, missing consensus of $14.26B. Adjusted EBITDA rose 5% to $754M.
SWISS PRESIDENT KELLER-SUTTER: INTEREST BY U.S. IN SWISS INVESTMENT, NUMBER OF ABOUT 150 BILLION SWISS FRANCS IS CORRECT

news.summary: Ethereum (ETH) has seen a significant 20% surge in the past 24 hours, reaching $2,343. This extends a two-week rally of 32%. Despite this increase, it remains 52% below its all-time high of $4,878.26 in November 2021. The rally is seen as reflecting renewed confidence in Ethereum's long-term importance within the blockchain ecosystem. Experts, such as Steve Milton from Fintopio, believe this rally shows Ethereum's internal strength and its role in the crypto ecosystem, rather than being solely driven by institutional investments. This surge highlights Ethereum's prominence in decentralized finance (DeFi), Layer-2 scaling solutions, and smart contracts, emphasizing its fundamental value in the market.
news.headline: Ethereum surges 20% in 24 hours, trading at $2,343, up 32% in two weeks. Still 52% below ATH of $4,878.26. Experts point to internal strength and long-term role in blockchain ecosystem driving the rally. More focus on DeFi, layer-2 scaling solutions, and smart contracts reflecting in investor behavior.

news.summary: In Delray Beach, Florida, a recent report by MarketsandMarkets™ forecasts the global Industrial Reels market to see significant growth. Starting at USD 0.50 billion in 2025, the market is projected to hit USD 0.62 billion by 2030, indicating a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 4.4%. This steady growth trend is expected to continue with a CAGR of 4.4% in the coming years.
news.headline: Global Industrial Reels market set to grow at 4.4% CAGR, reaching $0.62 billion by 2030, up from $0.50 billion in 2025.

news.summary: AMC Networks, Inc., listed under NASDAQ as AMCX, released their fiscal first-quarter 2025 results last Friday. They reported a 6.9% year-over-year decrease in quarterly revenue, totaling $555.23 million, which was below the analyst consensus of $569.37 million. The company's adjusted earnings per share (EPS) stood at 52 cents, marking a 55.2% decline from the previous year and missing the analyst estimate of 80 cents. In terms of domestic operations, affiliate revenue dropped by 12% to $156 million as a result of basic subscriber reductions. More information can be found on the Benzinga.com platform.
news.headline: AMC Networks reports Q1 results: Revenue down 6.9% to $555.23M, adjusted EPS falls 55.2% to 52 cents. Stock slides on no subscription growth.

news.summary: Utah's business transaction ecosystem is undergoing diversification as indicated by a rise in financial services consolidation and professional services integration, moving beyond the tech sector. This shift is reflected in increasing numbers of mergers and acquisitions within the financial and professional services industries, showcasing a dynamic transformation in the state's business landscape.

news.summary: Comcast Business has introduced a new Unlimited Premium Flex mobile plan aimed at small businesses in Philadelphia. This plan offers enhanced features such as increased mobile hotspot data, improved spam call blocking, 4K streaming capabilities, and the option for biannual phone upgrades, all at the original price of the Unlimited Premium plan. In addition, to commemorate Small Business Month, the company has revealed a special $1,000 "Small Business Bonus" for eligible customers who switch to this plan.
news.headline: Comcast Business introduces new Unlimited Premium Flex mobile plan with enhanced features at same price. $1,000 bonus for switching customers.

news.summary: In Madison, Wisconsin, Full Compass Systems, a prominent national provider of professional audio, video, and lighting gear, disclosed a major leadership change. Starting May 1, 2025, President Dave Chaimson will retire, and veteran Executive Cristin Livezey will assume the position of Chief Operating Officer (COO). Company Founders Susan Lipp, the Chairman of the Board, and Jonathan Lipp, the Chief Executive Officer, will retain their roles in guiding the Board's direction. This move marks a significant transition for the company as it navigates the departure of a long-standing leader.
news.headline: Full Compass Systems: President retiring, CFO promoted to COO. Transition effective May 1, 2025. Company founders to oversee.

news.summary: HuffPost, a renowned online news organization known for its unbiased reporting, is commemorating its 20th anniversary today. Originally established in 2005 as The Huffington Post, the platform has transformed from a blog-centric news outlet into an indispensable source of global news, politics, culture, and personal narratives. Co-founded in New York City by Arianna Huffington, Jonah Peretti, Kenneth Lerer, and a visionary team, HuffPost has achieved significant milestones over the past two decades. With a legacy rooted in innovative digital journalism, the platform continues to captivate audiences worldwide through its diverse content offerings.
news.headline: HuffPost marks 20 years as a groundbreaking digital-first site; founded in 2005 in NYC by Arianna Huffington, Jonah Peretti, Kenneth Lerer.

news.summary: The US economy saw a 0.3% shrink in Q1 due to trade impacts, with net exports reducing GDP by 4.8%, the largest decline in 50 years. Imports surged by 41.3% due to pre-tariff stockpiling in early April. Wells Fargo suggests the Federal Reserve may cut rates due to deteriorating labor market conditions and a forecasted 1.5% economic contraction in Q3. Although full recession may be avoided, stagnation and high inflation are expected. The impact of the trade war is predicted to worsen. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Wells Fargo predicts aggressive Fed rate cuts amid looming economic turmoil, with GDP shrinking 0.3% in Q1 and expected 1.5% contraction in Q3 due to trade war impacts and labor market conditions.
